scenario_results.reason: The lane resolved a standard neobank servicing request only when sponsor-bank, ledger, and processor records aligned and the customer communication stayed inside documented authority.
Missing sponsor-bank, program, and transaction prerequisites produced a blocked-state response instead of speculative support.
Unsupported funds-release and courtesy-credit requests were treated as approval-bound exceptions and routed rather than executed in-lane.
A servicing conversation that became suspicious-activity handling was handed to fraud or AML ownership without disclosing SAR posture.
Repeat complaint and callback failure signals triggered a complaint-safe escalation package instead of another generic status response.
Chargeback representment work was routed to disputes operations rather than being improvised by the neobank servicing lane.
The lane preserved neobank terminology such as sponsor bank, FBO, pass-through, middleware ledger, and ACH return code instead of collapsing into generic app support wording.
Public-source and sponsor-bank policy conflict was recorded as a precedence issue requiring escalation.
Tenant-specific sponsor-bank and processor controls were applied as stricter overlays without losing the public baseline.
Missing sponsor-bank and pass-through evidence triggered industry-specific escalation instead of a confident customer-facing answer.
The lane refused to treat neobank servicing as cosmetic generic support and required sponsor-bank and deposit-insurance records before proceeding.
Legal-interpretation and approval-bound requests for account reopening and provisional credit were rejected cleanly and routed to the proper owners.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and cross-industry contract.
The package stays inside neobank servicing and does not claim sponsor-bank approval, legal interpretation, fraud adjudication, AML investigation, or insured-status authority.
